content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 5, 1987


Sarah Clarenbach
Attorney at Law
8048 Soquel Drive
Aptos Village, CA  95003

Re:  Your Request for Assistance
     Our File No. A-87-199

Dear Ms. Clarenbach:

     You have written to us regarding the application of the
Political Reform Act to your client, the Aptos Incorporation
Study Group. Coincidentially, a very similar letter about the
same issues has also been received from Mr. Tom Connody of the
Aptos Incorporation Study Group. A copy of our response to his
letter is enclosed. I trust that this letter, together with a
copy of the Fontana Opinion, 2 FPPC Ops. 25 (No. 75-162,
February 4, 1976), will satisfactorily respond to your
questions.

     If you have any further questions or need further
assistance, please do not hesitate to contact this office. I
may be reached by phone at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Robert E. Leidigh
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

REL:plh
Enclosures




428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

SARA CLARENBACH
ATTORNEY AT LAW • ABOGADA
16346 SOQUAL DRIVE

Servicios Legales Bilingues

APTOS VILLAGE, CALIFORNIA 95003

Inglés—Español

Telephone
(408) 6861428

July 16, 1987


Diane Griffiths, Esq., Chief Counsel
Robert Leidigh, Esq., Senior Attorney
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:  Aptos Incorporation Study Group
     My File No. 0273

Dear Diane, Bob, or other Attorney:

     I write pursuant to my telephone conversation of July 8 with
Diane and July 9 with Bob regarding the above matter. As I told
you, I am working with the Aptos Incorporation Study Group, and
I am making this inquiry at its request. The Group is a loose
affiliation of people in the Aptos area, with no formal structure
at this point, which is studying the feasibility of incorporating
the community of Aptos.

     I am writing to request an opinion from your office as to
whether the Aptos Incorporation Study Group is an entity which
falls under the regulation of the Fair Political Practices Act.
In that regard, what if any rules pursuant to the Act might apply
to this Group?

     At this point, as I have indicated, the purpose of the Group
is simply to study the feasibility of incorporation, not to take
a position pro or con on incorporation. The Group wants to
collect money from various individuals in our area with the hope
of hiring a consultant to perform a study on feasibility of
incorporation. It is conceivable that, if the study comes back
favorable to incorporation, some of the people who are now
working with the loose Aptos Incorporation Study Group may decide
to support incorporation. At that point, whichever persons from
the Group, or persons who may not now be involved with the Group,
decide to support incorporation, would probably circulate
petitions and go through the requisite procedure with LAFCO and
other required agencies.

Diane Grittiths, Esq.
Robert Leidigh, Esq.
July 16, 1987

Page Two


A question of immediate concern is whether the Group can
start collecting money and putting it into a bank account so that
it can talk to consultants to get proposals for a feasibility
study. If you could render a quick opinion on that question to
begin with, we would much appreciate it, so that we can either
begin collecting money or do the proper paperwork to qualify the
Group to do so.

Please let me know if you need further factual background in
order to begin your work. As I understand it from Bob, there is
no charge for your service. If there is a fee for your work,
please let me know that before you begin any tasks. I look
forward to hearing from someone soon. Thank you for your
assistance.

Sincerely,

SARA CLARENBACH

SARA CLARENBACH
Attorney at Law

SC:dvm

cc    Ken Fleissner
      Terry Pershall
      Vic Marani
      Tom Carmody
      Kelly Walker
      Doug McConnell


***  Dictated but not read by Sara Clarenbach.

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 21, 1987


Sara Clarenbach
8048 Soquel Drive
Aptos Village, CA 95003

Re: 87-199

Dear Ms. Clarenbach:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on July 20, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Robert Leidigh, an attorney in
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
